Open a new Python environment or notebook (e.g., Google Colab) with Python 3.10 or compatible version
Install TensorFlow and Keras using pip without specifying Keras version explicitly (e.g., `pip install tensorflow keras`), so the latest Keras version is installed
Import the following modules: `import tensorflow as tf`, `from tensorflow import keras`, `import keras_cv`, `from tqdm.auto import tqdm`, `import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET`
Attempt to import `data_utils` from `keras.utils` by running `from keras.utils import data_utils`
Observe and capture the ImportError: `cannot import name 'data_utils' from 'keras.utils'`
To confirm the bug context, clone or download the Fine-tuning Stable Diffusion example code from https://keras.io/examples/generative/finetune_stable_diffusion/
Run the example script or notebook in the same environment with the latest installed Keras version
Verify that the ImportError related to `data_utils` occurs during the import phase, specifically within `keras_cv/models/weights.py` line containing `from keras.utils import data_utils`
Record the full error traceback to confirm the source and nature of the import failure
Uninstall the current Keras version using `pip uninstall keras`
Install Keras version 2.12.0 explicitly using `pip install keras==2.12.0`
Re-run the Fine-tuning Stable Diffusion example script or notebook
Verify that the ImportError no longer appears and the code runs past the previous import failure point
Assert that the only difference causing the import issue is the Keras version, showing that `data_utils` module is missing in latest Keras but present in version 2.12.0
Summarize the conditions reproducing the bug: latest Keras version + Fine-tuning Stable Diffusion example leads to ImportError on `data_utils` import
Conclude that the bug can be reproduced by running the example with the latest Keras version and verifying the ImportError
